title: Execute memory payload verification policy through primitives

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Route memory verify-payload JSON output through a codegen-owned generic payload verification primitive that consumes package-owned policy IR.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not embed Memory-specific file lists or product policy in command-generation; preserve the text-output runtime fallback; keep the slice bounded to memory verify-payload JSON execution and proof surfaces.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Primitive argument shape, schema names, generated freshness checks, and narrow parity tests needed to prove the JSON path.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The implementation requires porting full InstallResult text rendering, changing package lifecycle semantics, or expanding beyond verify-payload policy execution.
canonical_core.next_action: Implement payload.verify, wire memory.verify-payload JSON operation to it, regenerate projections, and prove parity/freshness.
proof_expectations: Primitive unit tests cover policy-driven verification without Memory-specific command-generation constants.
Memory installer policy parity tests prove the package-owned policy matches current product behavior.
Generated command package static and conformance checks prove the generated JSON path uses payload.verify and remains fresh.
completion_criteria: Execute memory payload verification policy through primitives is implemented, validated, and closed out honestly.

goal: Route memory verify-payload JSON output through package policy IR and codegen-owned primitive execution.
non_goals: Do not port full text formatting in this slice.
Do not claim all Memory lifecycle behavior is portable.
Do not add package-specific behavior to command-generation.
